document.title: TEST REPORT EN IEC 62368-1 Audio/video, information and communication technology equipment Part 1: Safety requirements

sourceSummary.oneLine: Dongguan Jun’an Testing and Certification issued report JAT24041102618SR-2 for SDAPO POE injector models tested to EN IEC 62368-1:2020+A11:2020.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports CE-LVD safety compliance evidence for the listed POE injector models under EN IEC 62368-1.
keyPoints: The tested samples comply with the requirements of EN IEC 62368-1:2020+A11:2020.
Test procedure is listed as CE-LVD.
European group differences were taken into account.
The test item description is POE injector with trade mark SDAPO.
Date of receipt of test item was 2024-04-01 and tests were performed from 2024-04-01 to 2024-04-10.

evidence.claim: The tested standard is EN IEC 62368-1:2020+A11:2020.
The test item is described as a POE injector.
The listed model/type references are PSE30, PSE801, PSE30G, PSE801G, PSE30TG, PSE802G, PSE60G, PSE156G, PSE90G, and POE4805.
The tested samples comply with EN IEC 62368-1:2020+A11:2020.
evidence.evidenceText: The tested samples comply with the requirements of EN IEC 62368-1:2020+A11:2020.

evidence.claim: European group differences were taken into account.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of compliance with National Differences European group differences were taken into account.
evidence.pageTarget: European group differences under EN IEC 62368-1

evidence.claim: The test item was received on 2024-04-01.
The equipment is connected to AC mains supply.
evidence.evidenceText: F.3.3.1 Equipment with direct connection to mains The equipment is connected to AC mains supply.

evidence.claim: The equipment is classified as IP20.
evidence.evidenceText: F.3.7 Equipment IP rating marking .............................. : This equipment is classified as IP20

evidence.claim: No battery is used in the equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: M.1 General requirements No such battery used. N/A

evidence.claim: The appliance coupler is considered the disconnect device.
evidence.evidenceText: L.1 General requirements The appliance coupler is considered as disconnect device.

evidence.claim: The equipment enclosure was evaluated as a fire enclosure.
evidence.evidenceText: 6.4.8 Fire enclosures and fire barriers Equipment enclosure was evaluated as a fire enclosure.

evidence.claim: No live parts can be accessible.
evidence.evidenceText: V.2 Accessible part criterion No live parts can be accessible. P

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the provided report text supports CE-LVD and European EN/CENELEC context rather than FDA.
Product description conflicts: the front matter identifies the test item as POE injector, while General Product Information states the equipment is a cash detector.
Some extracted tables appear inconsistent with a POE injector, including references to motor, display board, and cash-detector-like features.
No revision or version field for the report itself is present; revisionOrVersion is left empty.
OCR/text quality issues are present, including broken spacing, misspellings, and repeated inconsistent wording such as 'POE injector' used where disconnect device or cord may be intended.
